free_text: Nuclear charge dispersion of light-mass fission
products in the fission of U-235 and U-238 by medium-
Target material were U-235 (94%) and natural uranium
foils ranging in thickness from 77-122 mg/cm**2. The
target foils sandwiched between two aluminum catcher
foils (9 mg/cm**2) to stop recoiling fission fragments
from the target foil during the irradiation.
The copper foil, 21.4 mg/cm2 thick, was sandwiched
between two similar copper foils and placed upstream
Irradiation time varied from 8-min. to 20 min.
All the irradiations were performed at various
radii in the internal circulating proton beam.
The beam intensity varied between 0.5 to 1 muA.
Total isobaric cross section For a=93.
The activities of all nuclides were measured
The uncertainties quoted include both the
random errors associated with the decay curve analyses,
dilution factors, chemical yields, etc., as well as
efficiencies, spread in proton beam energy, etc.
* Errors due to uncertainties in the monitor cross
sections and decay-scheme data have not been  included.
In the case of duplicate measurements the errors quoted
are the mean deviations.  These were less than the
total uncertainty quoted, which varied between +-13 and
